title: Cowslips
content:
With rosy hand a little girl pressï¿½d down
A boss of fresh-cullï¿½d cowslips in a rill:
Often as they sprang up again, a frown
Showï¿½d she dislikï¿½d resistance to her will:
But when they droopï¿½d their heads and shone much less,
She shook them to and fro, and threw them by,
And trippï¿½d away. ï¿½Ye loathe the heaviness
Ye love to cause, my little girls!ï¿½ thought I,
ï¿½And what has shone for you, by you must die!ï¿½
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Cowslips"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
